channel.title: python on The Codex Arundel
channel.description: Recent content in python on The Codex Arundel

item.title: A Deeper Look At The `zip()` function
item.description: Introduction The zip() function in Python takes an input of two or more of the basic collections in Python; the list, set, tuple and dict. It returns an iterator (sequence) of tuples, where the first tuple contains the first elements of all the inputs, the second contains the second elements and so on. We’ll see how to use the Python zip() function in this article. Declaration Syntax for the zip() function is as follows:
